finding: Queue reconciliation previously validated commit-bound source inventory but still reconciled from mutable working-tree reads. The new adapter reads canonical queue, projections, and referenced evidence from one resolved commit, then computes separate source-inventory and candidate-state digests.

observed: The canonical queue, projections, and evidence are read through git object storage at one resolved commit.
Source and candidate digests are distinct.
The adapter never mutates canonical state and never derives authority from snapshot consistency.
inferred: Mixed-time working-tree reads are removed from the reconciliation path when this adapter is used.
proposed: Add an explicit signed or policy-bound canonical materialization authority contract.
superseded: Sequential working-tree reads can be treated as one coherent reconciliation snapshot.
unresolved: The tests are committed but no CI or local execution result is observed in this run.

test_cases: working-tree mutation cannot alter a commit-bound candidate
snapshot consistency cannot grant mutation authority

review_route: Execute the committed Node test, inspect both digests against the selected commit, then define materialization authority separately before any canonical queue mutation.
